configs.scenario: one constructor/dependent table, union b into a, rewrite f(b)->y, merge y into x on f(a) collision

observations: A displaced-id event is enough to find affected dependent rows through a reverse index.
The rewrite protocol emits only signed deltas for rows that are actually committed to live state.
A key collision on the rewritten dependent row is recorded as merge evidence and queues the resulting displaced-id event without emitting a non-live insertion.
Replaying row_deltas from initial_live_rows produces final_live_rows.
decision: Canonical-map rebuild can be modeled as a reverse-index collision protocol; the signed row-delta stream is replayable for committed live-row changes, while collision candidates are tracked separately until the protocol is mapped onto maintained DD traces.
limitations: This is an in-memory deterministic model, not a Differential Dataflow implementation.
The scenario covers one dependent-table collision shape and does not exercise multi-column keys or multiplicities greater than one.
next_action: Map the same displaced-id and signed-row-delta protocol onto the planned maintained-view/rebuild design.
